sections.paragraph:
ἀπο(ρ)ρίπτω fut. ἀπορρίψω LXX; 1 aor. ἀπέριψα (v.l. ἀπέρριψα Ac 27:43; W-S §5, 26b, B-D-F §11, 1). Pass.: 2 aor. ἀπερίφην Hv 3, 5, 5; 3, 6, 1; fut. ἀπορριφήσομαι LXX; pf. ἀπέρριμμαι LXX (s. ῥίπτω; Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; OdeSol 11:10; Test12Patr; JosAs; Philo, Ebr. 7; Joseph.; Just.; Tat. 20, 1).

① cause quick downward movement or separation away from a point or location, throw away in rejection (Jon 2:4) pass. ἀπὸ τοῦ πύργου Hs 9, 23, 3; cp. v 3, 5, 5; 3, 6, 1.

② to cause a sudden or forcible separation, drive/scare away, fig. ext. of 1 (Aeschyl. et al.; Himerius, Or. [Ecl.] 36, 1 τινὰ εἴς τι) μὴ ἀπορίψῃς με ἀπὸ τοῦ προσώπου σου do not drive me away fr. your presence 1 Cl 18:11 (Ps 50:13; gener. freq. in LXX; Jos., Bell. 1, 624 ἀ. ἀπό=drive away from.—ἀ.=reject, of God, in Celsus 3, 71.—Procop. Soph., Ep. 77 ἀπερριμμένοι=rejected ones; 94). μέριμναν ἀπορίψαντες casting care 1 Pt 5:7 P72. ἀ. ἀφʼ ἑαυτῶν πᾶσαν ἀδικίαν casting away fr. ourselves all unrighteousness 1 Cl 35:5 (cp. Ezk 18:31; 20:7f; SibOr 1, 338 ἀ. ἐκ κραδίης κακίας; of the casting off of a garment Jos., Bell. 1, 197, Ant. 6, 113; OdeSol 11:10; 1 Tat. 20:1).

③ propel oneself downward intr. (Lucian, Ver. Hist. 1, 30; Chariton 3, 5, 6; s. Moulton, ClR 20, 1906, 216) throw oneself down, jump Ac 27:43 (s. B-D-F §308; Rob. 797).—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v v = Visions

ext. ext. = extension

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

gener. gener. = generally

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

intr. intr. = intransitive

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

ClR ClR = Classical Review—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 119
